udt_struct_v0.doc: Ultra Light Node configuration for message verification.
udt_struct_v0.name: UlnConfig

fields.doc: Number of block confirmations required before message verification begins.
fields.name: confirmations

fields.doc: Minimum number of optional DVNs required to verify.
fields.name: optional_dvn_threshold

fields.doc: List of DVN addresses from which a threshold number must verify.
fields.name: optional_dvns

fields.doc: List of DVN addresses that must ALL verify the message (no threshold).
fields.name: required_dvns

udt_struct_v0.doc: OApp-specific ULN configuration with default override flags.
udt_struct_v0.name: OAppUlnConfig

fields.doc: OApp-specific ULN configuration (used when defaults are not applied).
fields.name: uln_config

fields.doc: Whether to use default confirmations.
fields.name: use_default_confirmations

fields.doc: Whether to use default optional DVNs.
fields.name: use_default_optional_dvns

fields.doc: Whether to use default required DVNs.
fields.name: use_default_required_dvns

udt_struct_v0.doc: Parameter for setting default ULN configuration for a destination/source endpoint.
udt_struct_v0.name: SetDefaultUlnConfigParam

fields.doc: The ULN configuration to set as default.
fields.name: config

fields.doc: The destination endpoint ID (for send) or source endpoint ID (for receive).
fields.name: eid

fields.doc: Address of the executor contract responsible for message execution.
fields.name: executor

fields.doc: Maximum size of messages that can be executed (in bytes).
fields.name: max_message_size

udt_struct_v0.doc: OApp-specific executor configuration.\n\nIf executor is `None`, the default executor is used.
udt_struct_v0.name: OAppExecutorConfig

fields.doc: Address of the executor contract to be used for message execution. None means use default configuration.
fields.name: executor

fields.doc: Maximum size of messages that can be executed (in bytes). 0 means use default configuration.
fields.name: max_message_size

udt_struct_v0.doc: Parameter for setting default executor configuration for a destination endpoint.
udt_struct_v0.name: SetDefaultExecutorConfigParam

fields.doc: The executor configuration to set as default.
fields.name: config

fields.doc: The destination endpoint ID.
fields.name: dst_eid

function_v0.doc: Sets default executor configurations for multiple destination endpoints.
function_v0.name: set_default_executor_configs

function_v0.doc: Sets default send ULN configurations for multiple destination endpoints.
function_v0.name: set_default_send_uln_configs

function_v0.doc: Returns the treasury address for fee collection.
function_v0.name: treasury

function_v0.doc: Returns the default executor configuration for a destination endpoint.
function_v0.name: default_executor_config

function_v0.doc: Returns the OApp-specific executor configuration for a destination endpoint.
function_v0.name: oapp_executor_config

function_v0.doc: Returns the effective executor configuration by merging OApp config with default.
function_v0.name: effective_executor_config

function_v0.doc: Returns the default send ULN configuration for a destination endpoint.
function_v0.name: default_send_uln_config

function_v0.doc: Returns the OApp-specific send ULN configuration for a destination endpoint.
function_v0.name: oapp_send_uln_config

function_v0.doc: Returns the effective send ULN configuration by merging OApp config with default.
function_v0.name: effective_send_uln_config

function_v0.doc: Quotes the total fee for sending a cross-chain message.\n\nCalculates fees from: executor (message execution), DVNs (verification), and treasury (protocol fee).
function_v0.name: quote

function_v0.doc: Sends a cross-chain message and returns fee recipients for payment.\n\nAssigns executor and DVNs, calculates fees, and encodes the packet for transmission.
function_v0.name: send

function_v0.doc: Called by a DVN to verify a message with a specific number of block confirmations.\n\nStores the DVN's verification attestation which will be checked during commit_verification.
function_v0.name: verify

function_v0.doc:
Permissionless function to commit a verified message to the endpoint after sufficient DVN verification.

Validates the packet header and checks that enough DVNs have verified the message
(all required DVNs + optional DVN threshold). Once verified, cleans up DVN confirmation
storage and calls the endpoint to mark the message as verified and executable.
function_v0.name: commit_verification

function_v0.doc: Sets default receive ULN configurations for multiple source endpoints.\n\nValidates each config and stores it as the default for the specified source EID.
function_v0.name: set_default_receive_uln_configs

function_v0.doc: Returns the number of block confirmations a DVN has submitted for a specific message.
function_v0.name: confirmations

function_v0.doc: Checks if a message has been sufficiently verified by DVNs and is ready to commit.
function_v0.name: verifiable

function_v0.doc: Returns the default receive ULN configuration for a source endpoint.
function_v0.name: default_receive_uln_config

function_v0.doc: Returns the OApp-specific receive ULN configuration override for a source endpoint.
function_v0.name: oapp_receive_uln_config

function_v0.doc: Returns the effective receive ULN configuration by merging OApp config with defaults.
function_v0.name: effective_receive_uln_config

function_v0.doc:
Sets OApp-specific configuration parameters for the message library.

Called by the endpoint on behalf of the OApp to configure executor and ULN settings.
Supports three config types: EXECUTOR, SEND_ULN, and RECEIVE_ULN.
function_v0.name: set_config

function_v0.doc: Returns the XDR-encoded effective configuration bytes.
function_v0.name: get_config

function_v0.doc: Returns true if the message library has full default configurations for the endpoint ID.
function_v0.name: is_supported_eid

function_v0.doc: Returns the version of the message library.
function_v0.name: version

function_v0.doc: Returns the type of the message library.
function_v0.name: message_lib_type

function_v0.doc: Returns the LayerZero endpoint contract address.
function_v0.name: endpoint

function_v0.doc: Returns the current owner address, or None if no owner is set.
function_v0.name: owner

function_v0.doc: Returns the pending owner address for 2-step transfer, or None if no transfer is pending.
function_v0.name: pending_owner

function_v0.doc:
Transfers ownership immediately to a new address.

Use with caution - if you transfer to a wrong address, ownership is lost forever.
Consider using `begin_ownership_transfer` instead.

# Panics
- `OwnerNotSet` if no owner is currently set
- `TransferInProgress` if a 2-step transfer is in progress
function_v0.name: transfer_ownership

function_v0.doc:
Begins an ownership transfer to a new address.

The new owner must call `accept_ownership()` within `ttl` ledgers
to complete the transfer. The pending transfer will automatically expire after.

# Arguments
- `new_owner` - The proposed new owner
- `ttl` - Number of ledgers the new owner has to accept.
Use `0` to cancel a pending transfer (new_owner must match pending).

# Panics
- `OwnerNotSet` if no owner is currently set
- `NoPendingTransfer` when cancelling and no pending transfer exists
- `InvalidTtl` if ttl exceeds max TTL
- `InvalidPendingOwner` when cancelling with wrong new_owner address
function_v0.name: begin_ownership_transfer

function_v0.doc:
Accepts a pending 2-step ownership transfer.

Must be called by the pending owner before the TTL expires.

# Panics
- `NoPendingTransfer` if there is no pending transfer (or it expired)
function_v0.name: accept_ownership

function_v0.doc:
Permanently renounces ownership.

# Panics
- `OwnerNotSet` if no owner is currently set
- `TransferInProgress` if a 2-step transfer is in progress (cancel it first)
function_v0.name: renounce_ownership

function_v0.doc:
Extends the instance TTL.

# Arguments

* `threshold` - The threshold to extend the TTL (if current TTL is below this, extend).
* `extend_to` - The TTL to extend to.
function_v0.name: extend_instance_ttl

function_v0.doc:
Sets TTL configs for instance and persistent storage.

- `None` values remove the corresponding config (disables auto-extension for that type)
- Validates that `threshold <= extend_to <= MAX_TTL`

# Arguments
- `instance` - TTL config for instance storage
- `persistent` - TTL config for persistent storage

# Panics
- `TtlConfigFrozen` if configs are frozen
- `InvalidTtlConfig` if validation fails
function_v0.name: set_ttl_configs

function_v0.doc: Returns the current TTL configs as (instance_config, persistent_config).
function_v0.name: ttl_configs

function_v0.doc:
Permanently freezes TTL configs, preventing any future modifications.

This is irreversible and provides immutability guarantees to users.
Emits `TtlConfigsFrozen` event.

# Panics
- `TtlConfigAlreadyFrozen` if already frozen
function_v0.name: freeze_ttl_configs

function_v0.doc: Returns whether TTL configs are frozen.
function_v0.name: is_ttl_configs_frozen
